tips: Kalimotxo was born in the 1970s; its origin is disputed between Puente la Reina (Navarre) and Getxo (Bizkaia).
In Basque it is kalimotxo; in Spanish, calimocho.
The classic mix is half red wine and half cola — every terrace has its own ratio.
It is served in a cachi glass, a small cup perfect for summer festivals and terraces.
A drink of northern popular culture: no fuss, just terrace and music.
In France it is known as calimocho; Italy has similar variants.
Like this app blending Wine and macOS, kalimotxo joins two unlikely worlds.
A symbol of the Spanish summer: village fiestas, neighbourhood bars and good vibes.
Not a cocktail-bar cocktail: young people invented it with whatever was in the fridge.
From Bilbao to Iruña, the cachi ritual marks the start of the night.
